steps.title: Spot the locational reference
steps.content: The opening sentence establishes a specific place—the Marie Hitchcock Puppet Theater in San Diego, California. When introducing a location this explicitly, the next sentence naturally references it.

steps.title: Apply the locational anchor
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification of place: the second sentence says what happens at the theater named in sentence one. The blank needs a transition signaling location/reference back to that place.
  • Choice A — "To that end,": signals purpose (sentence 2 is the goal of sentence 1), so fails — sentence 2 is not a goal, it is simply what happens there. ✗
  • Choice B — "However,": signals contrast, so fails — no opposing idea is introduced. ✗
  • Choice C — "Conversely,": signals direct opposition, so fails — the second sentence does not reverse anything. ✗
  • Choice D — "There,": signals location, referring back to the Marie Hitchcock Puppet Theater, so fits perfectly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
